title: mentha longifolia subsp. noeana
intro: Mentha longifolia subsp. noeana, also known as Bergamot Mint, is a subspecies of the Mentha longifolia plant, a type of mint native to the Mediterranean region. It is characterized by its distinctive orange or yellow flowers and its strong, citrusy scent. Bergamot Mint has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to alleviate stress, promote relaxation, and support overall well-being.
